critic_raw:
MNIST and fMNIST are near-linearly separable and contain essentially no spurious correlations between class labels and background features. The central claim of GCE — distinguishing causal from non-causal factors — is trivially easy on these datasets because there are almost no non-causal correlates to identify. The evaluation cannot demonstrate that GCE would identify the correct causal structure on data with genuine confounders.

'Causal explanation' as a concept requires a causal model (a DAG or structural equation model) as a precondition. The task description does not state whether Algorithm 1 uses a formally specified causal model of the data-generating process or whether it learns a latent factorization that is labeled 'causal' post-hoc. Without this distinction, the causal framing is unfalsifiable marketing.

Hardware specification (Nvidia GTX 1080) is cited as ensuring 'consistent performance outcomes.' Numerical reproducibility requires also fixing random seeds, framework versions, CUDA versions, and cuDNN determinism settings. Hardware identity alone does not control for non-deterministic GPU operations, so the reproducibility guarantee is incomplete.

No comparison against standard attribution baselines (SHAP, LIME, integrated gradients) is included. Without such comparisons, it cannot be determined whether the generative causal framing provides explanatory value beyond what non-causal feature attribution methods already provide.
all_issues_raised: MNIST and fMNIST have essentially no spurious correlations, making the core causal-vs-noncausal discrimination trivially easy and providing no evidence the method works on data with genuine confounders.
The task description does not clarify whether Algorithm 1 uses a formally specified causal model or learns a latent factorization labeled 'causal' post-hoc, making the causal framing unfalsifiable.
Hardware specification alone does not ensure numerical reproducibility; random seeds, framework versions, CUDA versions, and cuDNN determinism settings must also be fixed.
No comparison against standard non-causal attribution baselines (SHAP, LIME, integrated gradients) is provided, so the value added by the causal framing cannot be assessed.

The task description does not clarify whether Algorithm 1 uses a formally specified causal model or learns a latent factorization labeled 'causal' post-hoc, making the causal framing unfalsifiable.
Hardware specification alone does not ensure numerical reproducibility; random seeds, framework versions, CUDA versions, and cuDNN determinism settings must also be fixed.
No comparison against standard non-causal attribution baselines (SHAP, LIME, integrated gradients) is provided, so the value added by the causal framing cannot be assessed.
